original: Earthquake in Aleppo, Syria, kills an estimated 230,000 people
Treaty of Shaoxing ratified with Chinese southern Song Dynasty agreeing to pay tribute to northern Jin dynasty
Battle of Kappel: Swiss Roman Catholic cantons beat protestant forces of Zurich; Huldrych Zwingli Swiss reformation leader is killed
Holy Roman Emperor Charles V secretly names his son Philip as Duke of Milan, not made public till 1554
Battle of the South Seas: Dutch rebels defeat the Spanish navy
Adriaen Block and 12 Amsterdam merchants petition the States General for exclusive trading rights in the New Netherland colony
Burchardi flood, known as the second "Grote Mandrenke," kills about 15,000 in North Friesland, Denmark, and Germany
Hungary accepts Habsburg sovereignty
France, England & Netherlands ratified the First Partition Treaty, which eventually led to the War of the Spanish Succession

An earthquake is reported to have killed 300,000 and destroyed half of Calcutta in India, now thought to have been an exaggerated account of a hurricane that claimed 3,000 of the city's estimated 20,000 residents
Battle of Rocoux: French defeat British, Austrian and Dutch armies
Brigadier-General Arnold's Lake Champlain fleet defeated by British during the Battle of Valcour Island (American Revolutionary War)
Battle of Camperdown: British North Sea fleet led by Admiral Adam Duncan decisively defeats a Dutch fleet led by Vice-Admiral Jan de Winter, capturing eleven Dutch ships
John Stevens's Juliana, the first steam-powered ferry, begins service
Australia's oldest university, the University of Sydney, is inaugurated in Sydney
Battle of Dumfries, VA (Quantico Creek)
Skirmish at Rheatown/Henderson's Mill, Tennessee
Campina Grande, Brazil was established as a city
Slavery abolished in Maryland
Paul Bogle led hundreds of black men and women in a march in Jamaica, starting the Morant Bay rebellion
Toronto Argonaut Football Club 1st game losing to U of Toronto
American inventor David Houston (26) patents roll film for cameras
US and Canadian railroads agree to use of five time-zone system for North America, based on the Allegheny Observatory in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
A Miles patents elevator
Daughters of American Revolution founded
First 100 yard dash under 10 seconds run by John Owen in 9.8 sec at Analostan Island, Washington, D.C.
South African Boers declare war on Great Britain
Commencement of 1st Test Cricket between South Africa & Australia
San Francisco Board of Education orders segregation in separate schools of Japanese, Chinese, and Korean children sparking diplomatic crisis
German troops occupy Ghent
Bulgarian anti-Serbian offensive begins
Major tsumani shakes Caribbean
Armistice of Mudanya signed by Turkey and the Allies (Greece agrees 13 Oct) ending fighting in the Greco-Turkish War; Turkey to retain eastern Trace
First woman FBI "special investigator," Alaska Davidson, is appointed
German Mark falls to 10 billion per £, 4 billion per $
Bureau of Surrealist Research opens in Paris, directed by Antonin Artaud, resource centre for surrealist writers
Belgian episcopelian speaks against Flemish activism
NY Giants play their first NFL game, lose to Providence 14-0
Sean O'Casey's "Silver Tassle" premieres in London
Collingwood Football Club in Melbourne, Australia, wins the VFL premiership for the fourth consecutive year
100,000 extreme right-wing Germans form the "Harzburg Front"
First political telecast is made by the Democratic National Committee on CBS in NYC
"Professor Quiz" first radio quiz show premieres
NAACP organizes Legal Defense & Education Fund
-Oct 12] Naval battle at Cape Esperance, Guadalcanal
Allies bomb sea wall at Veere
Tuvinian People's Republic or formerly Tannu Tuva was annexed by the U.S.S.R
First man-made object to escape the atmosphere (WAC Corporal) is fired from White Sands Missile Range, New Mexico, reaching 235,000 feet
New York Yankees trade infielder Joe Gordon to Cleveland Indians for pitcherAllie Reynolds
Brazil & Chile break diplomatic relations with USSR
Costa Rica abolishes its army (decree 249)
The U.S. Federal Communications Commission issues the first license to broadcast television in color, to CBS
Persia signs Pact of Baghdad
1st Pakistan v Australia Test 95 runs scored on 1st day
AL President Will Harridge bars Washington Senators move to West Coast, unless unanimously approved by the other AL owners
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Maralinga, Australia
2nd US Moon probe, Pioneer 1, reaches 113,810 km, falls back
KTHI TV channel 11 in Fargo-Grand Forks, ND (NBC) begins broadcasting
Hurricane ravages East-Pakistan (6,000 die)
Radio/TV executive John Fetzer buys a controlling interest of Detroit Tigers
USAF Major Robert M White takes X-15 to 66,100m
USSR performs nuclear test
1st appearance of a Gabor sister on Merv Griffin Show
Apollo 7 (Schirra, Eisele & Cunningham) made 163 orbits in 260 hours
Coup d'état in Panama against President Arnulfo Arias
-28] Rome: 2nd bishop synod
Three people shot dead during street violence in the loyalist Shankill area of Belfast
Frank McGee becomes news anchor of Today Show
Switzerland recognizes North Vietnam
Panama adopts constitution
Prison uprising at Washington, D.C. jail
World Hockey Association officially debuts as the Alberta Oilers defeat the Ottawa Nationals, 7-4 at the Ottawa Civic Centre
Héctor José Cámpora is elected President of Argentina
MLB Oakland A's beat Baltimore Orioles 3 games to 2 to win AL pennant
Soyuz 25 returns to Earth
Aristides Royo elected president of Panama
Belgium government of Tindemans resigns
Columbia Records releases "52nd Street", singer-songwriterBilly Joel's sixth studio album; it becomes his 1st chart-topper, and wins 3 Grammy Awards
Allan McLeod Cormack & Godfrey Newbold Hounsfield win Nobel Prize for medicine for developing CAT scan
German DR frees dissidents Rudolf Bahro and Nico Hubner
Cosmonauts Popov and Ryumin set a space endurance record of 184 days
Dallas Mavericks open debut NBA season with a 103-92 victory against interstate rival San Antonio Spurs at Reunion Arena
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
LeRoy Irvin sets the record for yards gained on punt returns with 207 yards
Yanks beat Brewers 7-3 & win only Eastern Championship Series
English ship Mary Rose, which sank during an engagement with France in 1545, raised at Portsmouth, England
Riot in Amsterdam as tram catches fire
Last hand-cranked telephones US went out of service as 440 telephone customers in Bryant Pond, Maine, were switched over to direct-dial
Dr. Kathryn D. Sullivan completes the first spacewalk by an American woman
NASA launches space vehicle S-208
Atlantis (51-J) returns to Kennedy Space Center via Kelly AFB

200,000 march for gay and lesbian civil rights in Washington, D.C.
Center for Urban archaeology opens in NYC South Street Seaport Museum
Libya's Qadhafi says Israel must be eliminated
Mexican writer Octavio Paz is awarded the Nobel Prize for literature
Oil hits a record $40.42 per barrel
UK Foreign Secretary Douglas Hurd says force would be used if Iraq does not withdraw from Kuwait
Chip Beck ties PGA record lowest 18 hole score of 59 during 3rd round of the Las Vegas Invitational at the Sunrise GC
Toronto down 6-1 in 7th beats A's 7-6 in 11th in AL playoff
Norwegian Rushdie publisher William Nygaard injured in attack
US warship Harlan County anchors off Port-au-Prince Haiti
Russian ruble decreases to 3,926 rubles per dollar
Space shuttle STS-68 (Endeavour 7), lands
Ford buys rights to name Detroit domed stadium for $40 million
Congo Airlines Boeing 727 is shot down by rebels in Kindu, Democratic Republic of the Congo, killing 40 people
The 100th Space Shuttle mission (STS-92) is flown
The Polaroid Corporation files for federal bankruptcy protection
Bomb attack in a shopping mall in Vantaa, Finland, kills seven people
Nobel Prize in Literature is awarded to British novelist Doris Lessing
A further 83 people are killed throughout Syria by the Syrian army
A US appeal court overturns a district court ruling banning the sale of Samsung
10 people are killed and a hospital fire in Fukuoka prefecture, Japan
27 people are killed after a migrant boat sinks in the Channel of Sicily
The Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons wins the 2013 Nobel Peace Prize
Nepal's parliament elects Khadga Prasad Sharma Oli, leader of the Nepal-Unified Marxist Leninist party, as Prime Minister
South Korean firm Samsung announces it will permanently stop production of Note 7 phones after complaints devices caught fire
TripAdvisor customer poll names The Black Swan in Oldstead, North Yorkshire, world's best restaurant
Russian Soyuz spacecraft makes emergency landing when rocket fails two minutes after liftoff, with American astronaut and Russian cosmonaut aboard
Swedish dramatic soprano Nina Stemme is awarded the fourth $1 million Birgit Nilsson Prize
World's new longest flight flies from Singapore to Newark Airport, New Jersey takes 17 hours 52 mins
India records more than 7 million cases of COVID-19
Australia's largest city Sydney ends its 107-day lockdown, with restrictions easing for fully vaccinated people

Las Vegas Raiders head coach Jon Gruden steps down after The NY Times details emails in which he made homophobic and misogynistic remarks; earlier reports alleged racist statements about a union leader
Nobel Prize for Economics awarded to David Card, Joshua Angrist and Guido Imbens for their work on "natural experiments"
Wild beavers re-introduced to Ealing, London, from Scotland after being absence from the city for 400 years
Archaeologists announce the discovery of one of the oldest churches in the world from the 4th century BC, during excavations in Artaxata, Armenia

England beats Pakistan by an innings & 47 runs in 1st cricket Test in Multan; first time in Test history a side conceding 500 in 1st innings has gone on to win by an innings; 7-823 England's highest score since WWII

"Saturday Night Live" created byLorne Michaelspremieres on NBC with George Carlin as host
Islanders'Bryan Trottier's first career hat trick

US Vice Presidential candidates' debate -Geraldine Ferraro(D) and George H. W. Bush (R), in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Law Professor Anita Hill testifiesClarence Thomassexually harassed her

Deion Sandersplays for the Atlanta Falcons (NFL) in Miami and then flies to Pittsburgh, hoping to play for the Atlanta Braves (MLB) and become the first athlete to play in two pro sports on the same day; however, Braves manager Bobby Cox does not let him play
